# Code of Conduct

## Our Pledge

We as members, contributors, and leaders pledge to make participation in our community a harassment-free experience for everyone, regardless of age, body size, visible or invisible disability, ethnicity, sex characteristics, gender identity and expression, level of experience, education, socio-economic status, nationality, personal appearance, race, religion, or sexual identity and orientation.

We pledge to act and interact in ways that contribute to an open, welcoming, diverse, inclusive, and healthy community.

## Our Standards

Examples of behavior that contributes to a positive environment for our community include:

### General Conduct
- Demonstrating empathy and kindness toward other people
- Being respectful of differing opinions, viewpoints, and experiences
- Giving and gracefully accepting constructive feedback
- Accepting responsibility and apologizing to those affected by our mistakes
- Focusing on what is best not just for us as individuals, but for the overall community

### Cultural Sensitivity
- Respecting Tahitian culture, traditions, and language
- Acknowledging the sacred nature of traditional knowledge
- Seeking guidance from cultural experts when appropriate
- Using culturally appropriate language and imagery
- Crediting traditional knowledge sources properly

### Technical Collaboration
- Providing helpful and constructive code reviews
- Sharing knowledge and helping others learn
- Being patient with newcomers and different skill levels
- Documenting code and decisions clearly
- Following established coding standards and practices

## Unacceptable Behavior

Examples of unacceptable behavior include:

### Harassment and Discrimination
- The use of sexualized language or imagery, and sexual attention or advances of any kind
- Trolling, insulting or derogatory comments, and personal or political attacks
- Public or private harassment
- Publishing others' private information without their explicit permission
- Other conduct which could reasonably be considered inappropriate in a professional setting

### Cultural Insensitivity
- Misappropriation of Tahitian cultural elements
- Disrespectful use of traditional knowledge or sacred content
- Stereotyping or making assumptions about Tahitian culture
- Ignoring cultural guidance from community members
- Using cultural content without proper attribution

### Technical Misconduct
- Deliberately introducing bugs or security vulnerabilities
- Plagiarizing code without proper attribution
- Spamming issues or pull requests
- Disrupting community discussions
- Violating intellectual property rights

## Cultural Guidelines

Given TahitiSpeak's focus on Tahitian language and culture, we have additional guidelines:

### Respect for Traditional Knowledge
- Traditional stories, songs, and cultural practices are sacred
- Always seek permission before using traditional content
- Provide proper attribution to cultural sources
- Respect restrictions on certain cultural knowledge

### Language Accuracy
- Strive for accuracy in Tahitian language content
- Consult with native speakers when possible
- Acknowledge when content needs cultural review
- Be open to corrections from cultural experts

### Community Collaboration
- Welcome input from Tahitian community members
- Prioritize cultural authenticity over convenience
- Support efforts to preserve and promote the language
- Foster connections with Tahitian cultural organizations

## Enforcement Responsibilities

Community leaders are responsible for clarifying and enforcing our standards of acceptable behavior and will take appropriate and fair corrective action in response to any behavior that they deem inappropriate, threatening, offensive, or harmful.

Community leaders have the right and responsibility to remove, edit, or reject comments, commits, code, wiki edits, issues, and other contributions that are not aligned to this Code of Conduct, and will communicate reasons for moderation decisions when appropriate.

## Scope

This Code of Conduct applies within all community spaces, and also applies when an individual is officially representing the community in public spaces. Examples of representing our community include using an official e-mail address, posting via an official social media account, or acting as an appointed representative at an online or offline event.

## Enforcement

Instances of abusive, harassing, or otherwise unacceptable behavior may be reported to the community leaders responsible for enforcement at conduct@tahitispeak.com. All complaints will be reviewed and investigated promptly and fairly.

All community leaders are obligated to respect the privacy and security of the reporter of any incident.

## Enforcement Guidelines

Community leaders will follow these Community Impact Guidelines in determining the consequences for any action they deem in violation of this Code of Conduct:

### 1. Correction

**Community Impact**: Use of inappropriate language or other behavior deemed unprofessional or unwelcome in the community.

**Consequence**: A private, written warning from community leaders, providing clarity around the nature of the violation and an explanation of why the behavior was inappropriate. A public apology may be requested.

### 2. Warning

**Community Impact**: A violation through a single incident or series of actions.

**Consequence**: A warning with consequences for continued behavior. No interaction with the people involved, including unsolicited interaction with those enforcing the Code of Conduct, for a specified period of time. This includes avoiding interactions in community spaces as well as external channels like social media. Violating these terms may lead to a temporary or permanent ban.

### 3. Temporary Ban

**Community Impact**: A serious violation of community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior.

**Consequence**: A temporary ban from any sort of interaction or public communication with the community for a specified period of time. No public or private interaction with the people involved, including unsolicited interaction with those enforcing the Code of Conduct, is allowed during this period. Violating these terms may lead to a permanent ban.

### 4. Permanent Ban

**Community Impact**: Demonstrating a pattern of violation of community standards, including sustained inappropriate behavior, harassment of an individual, or aggression toward or disparagement of classes of individuals.

**Consequence**: A permanent ban from any sort of public interaction within the community.

## Cultural Mediation

For issues specifically related to cultural content or sensitivity:

### Cultural Advisory Process
- Cultural disputes will be reviewed by our Cultural Advisory Board
- Native Tahitian speakers and cultural experts will be consulted
- Traditional conflict resolution methods may be employed when appropriate
- Community elders may be invited to provide guidance

### Restorative Approach
- Focus on education and understanding rather than punishment
- Opportunity for learning about cultural sensitivity
- Community service within cultural preservation efforts
- Mentorship with cultural experts

## Reporting

### How to Report
- **General violations**: conduct@tahitispeak.com
- **Cultural sensitivity issues**: cultural@tahitispeak.com
- **Security concerns**: security@tahitispeak.com
- **Anonymous reporting**: Available through our website

### What to Include
- Description of the incident
- Date and time of occurrence
- People involved (if known)
- Any supporting evidence (screenshots, links, etc.)
- Impact on you or the community

### Response Timeline
- Acknowledgment within 24 hours
- Initial review within 72 hours
- Resolution within 2 weeks for most cases
- Complex cultural issues may require additional time for proper consultation

## Support Resources

### Community Support
- Mentorship program for new contributors
- Cultural guidance for non-Tahitian contributors
- Technical support for development questions
- Conflict resolution assistance

### External Resources
- Mental health support resources
- Cultural education materials
- Professional development opportunities
- Language learning resources

## Attribution

This Code of Conduct is adapted from the [Contributor Covenant][homepage], version 2.0, available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/version/2/0/code_of_conduct.html.

Community Impact Guidelines were inspired by [Mozilla's code of conduct enforcement ladder](https://github.com/mozilla/diversity).

Cultural guidelines were developed in consultation with Tahitian cultural experts and community leaders.

[homepage]: https://www.contributor-covenant.org

For answers to common questions about this code of conduct, see the FAQ at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/faq. Translations are available at https://www.contributor-covenant.org/translations.

## Contact

For questions about this Code of Conduct:

- Cultural questions: cultural@tahitispeak.com
- General inquiries: hello@tahitispeak.com
